title: Dracophyllum pandanifolium
taxonomy: Dracophyllum pandanifolium belongs to the family Ericaceae. It is a species of flowering plant in the genus Dracophyllum. The genus Dracophyllum is part of the order Ericales and is native to Australia. Dracophyllum pandanifolium is a shrub that is commonly found in eastern Australia. The classification of Dracophyllum pandanifolium reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.

intro: Dracophyllum pandanifolium, also known as the prickly dracophyllum, is a small, shrub-like plant native to the Australian outback. The plant has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its potential antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Dracophyllum pandanifolium include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infusion, or made into a tincture or powder for use in treating various health ailments.
